mnemonics.title: Walk Types Hierarchy
mnemonics.content: Walk → Trail (no edge repeat) → Path (no vertex repeat) → Cycle (path + returns to start). Think: WTTPC - Walk Then Trail Then Path Cycle

mnemonics.title: Isomorphism Checklist
mnemonics.content: For graphs to be isomorphic: Same #vertices, Same #edges, Same degree sequence, Same connectivity. Use: VEC - Verify Equality of Count

mnemonics.title: Subgraph Types
mnemonics.content: Spanning: All vertices, some edges. Induced: All vertices in subset + ALL edges between them. Remember: SI - Spanning Includes all vertices, Induced includes all edges between induced vertices

acronyms.usage: Isomorphism verification checklist - all must match
Hierarchy of walk types with increasing constraints

acronyms.expansion: Spanning INcludes all vertices, Induced includes all Edges between selected vertices
acronyms.usage: Remembering difference between spanning and induced subgraphs

visualTips.visualization: Imagine two graphs drawn differently but when you relabel vertices of one, they become identical. Like same network topology drawn in different layouts.
Think of a city map: Walk = any route possibly retracing streets; Path = direct route no retracing; Cycle = return trip to start.
Spanning = keep all cities but close some roads; Induced = pick some cities and keep ALL direct roads between them.

analogies.analogy: Two companies with different names but identical organizational structure - same positions and reporting relationships.
Walking through a mall with possible backtracking - you can enter and exit stores multiple times.
One-way street system - you visit each location exactly once without retracing.
Sightseeing bus tour that returns to starting point - covers various attractions without repeating edges.
Selecting a group of friends from a social network - you automatically connect all friends who know each other within that group.
